question.text: There are three types of Clearing Members in India's Exchange-Traded Currency Derivatives market. Which of the following Clearing Member types has ONLY clearing rights and NO trading rights?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Professional clearing member: They have only clearing rights and do not have trading rights. They clear and settle trades executed by trading members and custodial participants." **The Trap:** TCM and SCM both have trading AND clearing rights. PCM is distinctive because it only clears — it relies on trading members to generate the trades it then clears. **Cross-Exam Context:** Three CM types: SCM = trades only for itself (no external CPs); TCM = trades for itself AND other TMs/CPs; PCM = clears only, no trading. SEBI-registered custodians and banks can become PCMs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PCM = "Pure Clearing" Member — no trading, just clearing. The "Professional" label reflects their specialist clearing-only role.
